heading: Delete IP Access List Entries
body:
> **Important:** 
>
> When you remove an entry from the IP access list, existing connections
> from the removed addresses may remain open for a variable amount of
> time. How much time passes before Atlas closes the connection
> depends on several factors, including:
>
> - how the connection was established
>
> - how the application or [driver](drivers) using the address behaves
>
> - which protocol (like TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) or UDP (User Datagram Protocol)) the connection uses**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo delete an IP access list from your project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas accessLists delete <entry> [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas accessLists delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-accessLists-delete).**Api:**Atlas Administration APIYou can use the Atlas Administration API to [delete
existing users](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deleteprojectipaccesslist).**Ui:**Atlas UIIf it isn't already displayed, click the
IP Access List tab.
